content:
Committed and pushed `90839b0f` to PR #89 (`88-chat-inline-send-icon`).

**What changed** (`src/dashboard/lib/components/KodyChat.tsx`):
- Added `Send` to lucide-react imports
- Removed the standalone **Start** and **Send** button group (the `composerAction` block)
- Added a `Send` icon to the toolbar row (right side, after VoiceButton) — visible only when input has text
- Click handler boots the runner if idle, sends otherwise; Stop/Cancel buttons preserved for their states

The PR stays draft. Vercel is redeploying the preview now — should be ready shortly at the same preview URL.

[Dashboard context — the user is currently viewing the Vibe page (/vibe) in the Kody dashboard. When they say "this page", "here", or ask what they're looking at, they mean this.]

[Vibe mode — follow-up on an existing issue, do not echo this block]

I'm iterating on issue #88 and PR #89. The dashboard chat agent already opened a draft PR for this issue against a vibe branch — Vercel is cold-building it in parallel with your warm-up. Read the existing issue body, the current diff, and the latest preview state before answering.

Workflow:
1. Use the existing branch `88-chat-inline-send-icon` for all commits.
2. Research what's already shipped: run `gh issue view 88`, `gh pr view 89 --json files,headRefName,body`, and read the files the PR touches. Understand what was already done.
3. Reply with a short plan for the requested change (what files, what edits, why). Ask me to confirm before editing — UNLESS my message explicitly says to proceed without confirmation (e.g. "the plan was approved in the previous chat", "do not ask for confirmation again", "implement now and skip confirmation"). In that case, skip the plan-and-ask step and go straight to step 5.
4. Do NOT create a new issue or a new branch — push the follow-up commits onto the vibe branch from step 1 so the existing draft PR updates and Vercel redeploys the same preview URL.
5. On my confirmation (or immediately when the message says to skip confirmation), check out that branch, make the edits, commit with a clear message, push to origin, and reply with the commit SHA + a short summary of what changed. The PR stays a draft until I mark it ready.
6. If the user's request seems unrelated to the current issue (a new feature, not a fix to this one), say so and ask whether to fork a new vibe session instead.

## HARD RULE — never end a turn with uncommitted changes

This runner's filesystem is ephemeral. Any edit you make is LOST when this
turn ends unless you've committed and pushed it. So:

- After any edit, before you send your reply, stage your code changes with
  pathspec exclusions so the dashboard's chat plumbing doesn't leak into
  the PR:
  ```
  git add -A -- ':!.kody/sessions' ':!.kody/events'
  git status
  ```
  `.kody/sessions/*.jsonl` and `.kody/events/*.jsonl` are the live chat
  transcript + event stream the dashboard writes mid-turn — they belong on
  `main`, never on a feature branch. The exclusion above is mandatory; do
  NOT run a bare `git add -A` or `git add .` in vibe mode.
- Verify the working tree only contains files you intend to leave
  uncommitted (e.g. node_modules artifacts). If `.kody/sessions/` or
  `.kody/events/` still show up in `git status`, you forgot the exclusion —
  go back and stage with the pathspec above.
- Run `git log -1 --oneline` and `gh pr view <pr> --json headRefOid` to
  confirm the commit you just made is on the PR branch on the remote.
- Your reply MUST cite the commit SHA you just pushed. If you cannot cite
  a SHA, you have not finished — push first, then reply.
- Never say things like "I've made the changes" / "updated the file" /
  "changes are ready" unless you have just pushed and have a SHA to show.
  Uncommitted edits in vibe mode = zero changes from the user's perspective.

My actual request follows below.
---
Can you check the status? Nothing happened.
